text: In a laparoscopic right hemicolectomy on an obese male patient, the surgeon opens the mesenteric window at the inferior edge of the ileocolic vascular pedicle. The ileocolic vessels are identified and relegated. The inferior part of the omentum is exposed first, followed by the ansinate process of the pancreas. Lymph node dissection is performed along the superior mesenteric vein from the origin of the ileocolic vein to the gastrocolic trunk of Henle's surgical area.

The surgeon carefully exposes the superior right colic vein from the medial side, using it as an anatomical landmark for the oncological right hemicolectomy. Lymph nodes are dissected around the superior right colic vein and the right branch of the middle colic vessels, with attention to vascular variability and fragility to minimize intraoperative bleeding risks.
